patch.value: GH-280: pm validate does not detect circular parent references (hierarchy cycles A->B->A of any length)

pm validate lifecycle check detects blocked_by/definition_of_ready dependency cycles via Tarjan SCC (findLifecycleDependencyCycleComponents, #271) but the parent (composition) graph is never traversed, so a parent cycle reports dependency_cycle_count:0 with no warning while silently breaking pm list --tree, orphan detection, and progress rollups. Fix: add a parent-hierarchy cycle check (reuse the generic Tarjan helper on a child->parent adjacency map), emit validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle warning + offending ids/sample path, severity policy (off|warn|error) mirroring dependency-cycle policy, remediation registry entry, and --check selection support.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
Root cause: pm validate detects blocked_by/definition_of_ready dependency cycles via Tarjan SCC (findLifecycleDependencyCycleComponents) but NEVER builds an adjacency map over the `parent` composition graph. A parent cycle (A.parent=B, B.parent=A, or longer) reports dependency_cycle_count:0 with no warning, silently breaking pm list --tree, orphan detection, and progress rollups.

Design decisions:
(1) ALL-ITEMS not active-only: parent cycles are structural corruption regardless of status (a cycle among closed items still breaks tree/rollups), and the existing terminal-parent check already cares about parent edges to terminal items. So buildLifecycleParentGraph iterates ALL `items` (not the activeItems filter the dependency-cycle path uses). Edges guarded to known item ids (matches dependency-graph dangling-ref guard).
(2) FOLD into existing lifecycle check details (parent_cycle_*) + reuse findLifecycleDependencyCycleComponents/resolveLifecycleDependencyCycleSamplePath/summarizeList — avoids a new ValidateCheckName and contract churn. New warning codes validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle / validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle_error. Lifecycle check status=error when parentCycleSeverity=error AND a cycle exists.
(3) New --parent-cycle-severity <off|warn|error> flag (default warn), resolveParentCycleSeverity resolver.

Reusing the GENERIC Tarjan helper directly on a child->[parent] map (single-element edge arrays).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text:
DONE. Implementation summary:

src/cli/commands/validate.ts: added resolveParentCycleSeverity (mirrors resolveDependencyCycleSeverity, default warn, USAGE error on invalid), buildLifecycleParentGraph (child->[parent] adjacency over ALL items, guarded to known ids + toMeaningfulString), detectLifecycleParentCycles (reuses generic findLifecycleDependencyCycleComponents + resolveLifecycleDependencyCycleSamplePath). buildLifecycleCheck gained a parentCycleSeverity param, emits validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle / _error warnings, folds parent_cycle_severity_policy/count/item_count/item_ids[+_truncated]/sample_paths[+_truncated] into the SAME lifecycle check details, and status=error when either dependency- OR parent-cycle severity=error and a cycle exists (hasErrorSeverityCycle). Added parentCycleSeverity to ValidateCommandOptions + runValidate wiring. Exported the 3 new fns on _testOnlyValidateCommand.

No new ValidateCheckName (folded into lifecycle) -> zero check-selection churn.

Contract/flag surfaces: register-operations.ts (--parent-cycle-severity option + threading), src/sdk/cli-contracts.ts VALIDATE_FLAG_CONTRACTS, help-content.ts example, completion.ts (bash compgen list + zsh + fish), tests/fixtures/contracts/full.json regenerated via pnpm contracts:update (build first, then update). MCP pm_validate uses generic options:{} schema (no per-option parameter table) -> no MCP change needed, same as dependencyCycleSeverity.

remediation.ts: added validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle_error (FIRST) + validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle entries -> command 'pm update <id> --parent <other-id>'. Order matches the existing dependency_cycle _error-first pattern so colon-boundary mutual-exclusivity holds.

Tests (validate-command.spec.ts): seedParentCycle(context, 2|3) helper (creates tasks then pm update --parent ring); helper-edge assertions for resolveParentCycleSeverity/buildLifecycleParentGraph (dangling/none/blank guards)/detectLifecycleParentCycles; 5 integration tests: two-item A->B->A, three-item A->B->C->A, severity error/off overrides, no-false-positive on acyclic root->child->grandchild, rejects unknown severity. Updated existing buildLifecycleCheck direct-call (added positional arg) + completion-command.spec expected bash string.

Results: pnpm typecheck PASS; validate spec 82 pass; remediation-registry 17 pass; completion/contracts/register/help specs pass; pnpm quality:static PASS; pnpm contracts:check current; coverage of validate.ts AND remediation.ts = 100/100/100/100 (no new c8-ignore). Verified live via dist CLI: warn->status warn + validate_hierarchy_parent_cycle:1; error->ok:false + status error + _error:1 + fix_hint 'pm update <id> --parent <other-id>'. Not committed/pushed.
